skill.name: architecture-documenter
skill.description: Create clear system architecture documents, technical decision records, and diagrams for software teams.
skill.summary: Documents architecture, design trade-offs, diagrams, and ADRs for software systems.
skill.author: CuriousLearner

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, environment, storage, and cryptography patterns. Manual review found these are Markdown examples and architecture templates, not executable skill behavior. The remaining concern is low: users may include sensitive infrastructure details in generated documentation if they do not sanitize inputs.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick findings occur inside Markdown code fences and JavaScript template literal examples. They do not instruct the assistant to run shell commands or execute user-controlled input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is fenced documentation showing cache keys and JWT example code. I found no active script file or instruction that executes these snippets.

low_findings.title: Network, Storage, And Environment Findings Are Illustrative
low_findings.description: Hardcoded private CIDR ranges, reference URLs, secure storage terms, and process.env usage appear in architecture and ADR examples. They are not active network requests, credential reads, or browser storage access by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is embedded in documentation templates and external reference links. There is no code path that sends data over the network or reads runtime environment secrets.

low_findings.title: Architecture Documentation Can Capture Sensitive Details
low_findings.description: The skill encourages documenting network topology, security architecture, token handling, and operational controls. This is useful, but users should remove secrets and production-only details before sharing generated documents.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The risk is semantic rather than executable. The templates ask for security and infrastructure details, which can be sensitive if copied from production without redaction.

content.value_statement: Architecture knowledge often becomes scattered across code, chats, and outdated diagrams. This skill helps teams create structured architecture documents, ADRs, and diagram plans that explain systems clearly.
actual_capabilities: Creates system architecture document outlines with context, components, data flows, infrastructure, and operations sections.
Structures architecture decision records with context, decision, alternatives, consequences, and status.
Recommends diagram types such as system context, component, sequence, deployment, and data flow diagrams.
Documents security, scalability, reliability, monitoring, and deployment considerations.
Helps update living architecture documentation when systems or decisions change.
limitations: It relies on the architecture details supplied by the user or available project files.
It does not verify production infrastructure, runtime behavior, or cloud configuration automatically.
It may need human review for business context, compliance claims, and sensitive details.
It provides documentation structure, not executable diagram rendering by itself.

use_cases.title: Document A New Service
use_cases.description: Create a clear architecture overview before implementation starts, including components, dependencies, data flow, and deployment assumptions.

use_cases.title: Record A Technical Decision
use_cases.description: Capture why a team chose a database, authentication model, deployment strategy, or integration approach.

use_cases.title: Refresh Outdated System Docs
use_cases.description: Update existing architecture notes after migrations, scaling changes, security changes, or new operational requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Create A Basic Overview
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: start documentation for a known application.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use architecture-documenter to create a system overview for this application. Include users, major components, dependencies, data stores, and open questions.

prompt_templates.title: Draft An ADR
prompt_templates.prompt: Use architecture-documenter to write an ADR for choosing this approach. Include context, decision, alternatives considered, trade-offs, consequences, and review date.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Diagrams
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: prepare diagrams for team review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use architecture-documenter to recommend the diagrams needed for this system. Describe each diagram, its audience, and the information it should show.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Living Documentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use architecture-documenter to review these architecture docs against the current codebase. Identify stale sections, missing decisions, unclear boundaries, and sensitive details to redact.

output_examples.input: A team needs documentation for a payment service that talks to checkout, billing, and a payment provider.
output_examples.output: A system architecture outline with service responsibilities, dependency map, data ownership notes, failure modes, security considerations, and diagram recommendations.

output_examples.input: A team chose JWT authentication instead of server sessions and needs to record the decision.
output_examples.output: An ADR that explains the context, selected approach, rejected alternatives, security trade-offs, rollout plan, and future review triggers.

output_examples.input: Existing architecture docs are out of date after a migration to cloud hosting.
output_examples.output: A documentation update plan covering infrastructure, deployment flow, monitoring, scalability assumptions, and sections that need owner review.

best_practices: Provide current system facts, known constraints, and the intended audience before asking for documentation.
Review generated docs for secrets, internal hostnames, credentials, and production-only operational details.
Store final architecture docs with the code or team documentation system so they stay versioned.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to invent architecture details that the team has not confirmed.
Do not publish generated security diagrams without redacting sensitive infrastructure information.
Do not treat an ADR as final until stakeholders review the trade-offs and consequences.

faq.question: What does this skill produce?
faq.answer: It produces architecture document outlines, ADRs, diagram plans, and guidance for documenting system design decisions.

faq.question: Can it create diagrams directly?
faq.answer: It can describe useful diagrams and provide structure for them. Rendering depends on the tools available in your environment.

faq.question: Does it inspect my infrastructure automatically?
faq.answer: No. It relies on project files and details you provide. Verify all infrastructure claims before publishing.

faq.question: Is it suitable for security architecture?
faq.answer: Yes, it includes security architecture sections. Remove sensitive details before sharing documents broadly.

faq.question: Can it update existing documentation?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide the current docs and the system changes, then ask it to identify stale sections and required updates.

faq.question: Who should review the output?
faq.answer: A technical owner should review accuracy, and a security or operations owner should review sensitive details.
